title: Modern Kids Bookrack
summary: A small angled bookrack on three matching 2x2 A-frame stands, holding books face-out on a slanted 1x4 ledge between a 1x10 back and a 1x12 base.
description:
Each stand is a small 2x2 A-frame — a straight upright, an angled back leg, and two short feet, all cut with matching 10° angles so all three stands sit and lean identically. Build all three the same way before attaching anything else, since any mismatch between them shows immediately once the shelf boards go on.

The 1x10 boards form the angled back panel that books lean against, and the 1x12 boards form the base ledge books rest on, both spanning across all three A-frame stands. **Books display face-out here, not spine-out** — that's the whole design idea, and it's why the back panel is angled rather than vertical.

Six 1x4 boards cap the front edge of the base ledge, giving books something to stop against so they don't slide forward off the rack.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 10° off square, parallel.
One end cut at 10° off square, longest point measurement.
One end cut at 10° off square, longest point measurement.

steps.title: Build the three A-frame stands
steps.body: Cut the angled pieces and build three identical A-frame stands from the uprights, angled back legs, and feet.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Attach the two 1x10 back panel boards across the angled back legs of all three stands.

steps.title: Attach the base ledge
steps.body: Attach the two 1x12 base ledge boards across the front of the stands, below the back panel.

steps.title: Add the front lip
steps.body: Attach the six 1x4 lip boards along the front edge of the base ledge to stop books from sliding off.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Modern kids bookrack displaying books face out
